title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
summary: PostHog is developing a self-driving products pipeline that transforms product signals such as rage clicks, errors, and user messages into pull requests via background agents. The system groups related errors and session replays, researches the codebase, and opens PRs automatically.
writeup: Joshua Snyder of PostHog presents a self-driving products pipeline that converts product signals such as rage clicks, errors, and user messages into pull requests through background agents. The system groups related errors and session replays, researches the codebase, and opens PRs automatically. It demonstrates how agentic workflows can close the loop from observed user friction to proposed code changes without manual triage. Supporting concerns include test-time compute and cost optimization across the agent runs.
